name: glview

metadata.name: glview
metadata.summary: Lightning-fast image viewer with smooth zooming & panning.

documentation:
# glview

[![Build Status](https://travis-ci.com/toaarnio/glview.svg?branch=master)](https://travis-ci.com/github/toaarnio/glview)

Lightning-fast image viewer with synchronized split-screen zooming & panning + HDR & WCG support

**Installing:**
```
pip install glview
```

**Usage:**
```
glview --help
```

**Building & installing from source:**
```

modules.name: glview
documentation:
Lightning-fast image viewer with smooth zooming & panning.

This package provides the 'glview' command-line application
only. There are no Python modules that you could import and
use in your own application.

https://github.com/toaarnio/glview

modules.name: glview.imageprovider
documentation: A multithreaded image file loader.

classes.name: glview.imageprovider.ImageProvider

methods.name: glview.imageprovider.ImageProvider.__init__
documentation: Create a new ImageProvider with the given (hardcoded) FileList instance.

methods.name: glview.imageprovider.ImageProvider.start
documentation: Start the image loader thread.

methods.name: glview.imageprovider.ImageProvider.stop
documentation: Stop the image loader thread.

methods.name: glview.imageprovider.ImageProvider.validate_files
documentation: Quickly validate all image files & estimate their memory consumption.

methods.name: glview.imageprovider.ImageProvider.get_image
documentation:
Return the image at the given index. The image might not be present,
in which case the return value is either "PENDING" (not loaded yet),
"RELEASED" (already released to free up the memory), or "INVALID"
(tried to load the image but failed).

methods.name: glview.imageprovider.ImageProvider.release_image
documentation: Release the image at the given index to (eventually) free up the memory.

methods.name: glview.imageprovider.ImageProvider._degamma
documentation:
Apply standard sRGB inverse gamma on the given uint8/16 frame, returning
the result in normalized float16 format.

methods.name: glview.imageprovider.ImageProvider._sequential_loader
documentation: Load all images in sequential order.

methods.name: glview.imageprovider.ImageProvider._load_single
documentation:
Read image, drop alpha channel, convert to fp32 if maxval != 255;
if loading fails, mark the slot as "INVALID" and keep going.
A multithreaded image file loader.

modules.name: glview.argv
documentation:

!/usr/bin/env -S python3 -B

functions.name: glview.argv.filenames
documentation:
Examples:
  filenames = argv.filenames(sys.argv[1:], numRequired=2)
  filenames = argv.filenames(sys.argv[1:], [".ppm", ".png"])
  filenames = argv.filenames(sys.argv[1:], [".jpg"], allowAllCaps=True)
pylint: disable=invalid-name
P U B L I C   A P I

functions.name: glview.argv.exists
documentation:
Example:
  showHelp = argv.exists("--help")

functions.name: glview.argv.intval
documentation:
Example:
  numtiles = argv.intval("--split", 1, [1, 2, 3, 4])

functions.name: glview.argv.floatval
documentation:
Example:
  factor = argv.floatval("--factor", 1.0, condition='1.0 <= v <= 3.0')

functions.name: glview.argv.stringval
documentation:
Examples:
  bayer = argv.stringval("--bayer", default="AUTO", accepted=["AUTO", "GBRG", "RGGB"])
  sources = argv.stringval("--source", default="all", accepted=["foo", "bar", "baz", "all"], repeats=True)

functions.name: glview.argv.intpair
documentation:
Examples:
  width, height = argv.intpair("--size", default=(1920, 1080))
  limits = argv.intpair("--limits", default=(0, 1), repeats=True)

functions.name: glview.argv.floatpair
documentation:
Example:
  factor1, factor2 = argv.floatpair("--factors", default=(1.0, 1.0))

functions.name: glview.argv.floatstring
documentation:
Examples:
  blacklevel = argv.floatstring("--whitelevel", "AUTO", accepted=["AUTO"])
  whitelevel = argv.floatstring("--blacklevel", 1023.0, accepted=["AUTO"])

functions.name: glview.argv.exitIfAnyUnparsedOptions
documentation:
Example:
  factor = argv.floatval("--factor", default=1.0)
  showHelp = argv.exists("--help")
  argv.exitIfAnyUnparsedOptions()

functions.name: glview.argv._enforce
documentation: If 'expression' is False, prints out the given error message and exits.
I N T E R N A L   F U N C T I O N S

functions.name: glview.argv._string
documentation: Parses arguments of the form '--argname string', returns the string.

functions.name: glview.argv._isInt
documentation: Returns True if and only if the given string represents an integer.

functions.name: glview.argv._isFloat
documentation: Returns True if and only if the given string represents a float.

functions.name: glview.argv._isValid
documentation: Checks that 'arg' is in validArgs and satisfies the given condition.

classes.name: glview.argv._Tests

methods.name: glview.argv._Tests.test_exists
documentation: pylint: disable=missing-docstring
U N I T   T E S T S

modules.name: glview.glrenderer
documentation: A tiled image renderer with zoom & pan support based on OpenGL.

classes.name: glview.glrenderer.GLRenderer

methods.name: glview.glrenderer.GLRenderer.__init__
documentation:
Create a new GLRenderer with the given (hardcoded) PygletUI, FileList, and
ImageProvider instances.

methods.name: glview.glrenderer.GLRenderer.init
documentation: Initialize an OpenGL context and attach it to an existing window.

methods.name: glview.glrenderer.GLRenderer.redraw
documentation: Redraw the tiled image view with refreshed pan & zoom, filtering, etc.

methods.name: glview.glrenderer.GLRenderer.upload_texture
documentation:
Upload the given image to GPU memory, either all at once or piecewise
(100 rows per call). Progressive uploading helps avoid freezing the
user interface, although short glitches may still occur with large
images.

methods.name: glview.glrenderer.GLRenderer.screenshot
documentation:
Render the current on-screen view into an offscreen buffer and return the image
as a NumPy array.

methods.name: glview.glrenderer.GLRenderer._gamut
documentation:
Calculate per-color-channel scale factors as required by the shader for gamut
compression. The calculation is based on three user-defined parameters (power,
limit, and threshold) that control the shape of the compression curve. Per-image
control of the 'limit' parameter is supported, but not currently used.

methods.name: glview.glrenderer.GLRenderer._gamut_curve
documentation:
Calculate a curve shaping scale factor for each color channel, based on the given
power, threshold and limit.

Arguments:
  - power: curve exponent; higher value = steeper curve
  - thr: percentage of core gamut to leave unmodified
  - lim: upper bound for values to compress into gamut

Returns:
  - scale: compression curve global scale factor, required by the shader

class_variables.name: glview.glrenderer.GLRenderer.filters
documentation: noqa: RUF012

class_variables.name: glview.glrenderer.GLRenderer.tile_debug_colors
documentation: pastel shades
A tiled image renderer with zoom & pan support based on OpenGL.

modules.name: glview.glview
documentation: The 'glview' command-line application.
!/usr/bin/python3 -B

functions.name: glview.glview.main
documentation: Parse command-line arguments and run the application.

functions.name: glview.glview.main_loop
documentation: Keep the application running until exit request or out of memory.

functions.name: glview.glview.enforce
documentation: Display the given error message and exit if 'expression' is False.

functions.name: glview.glview.warn
documentation: Display the given warning message if 'expression' is True.

classes.name: glview.glview.FileList

methods.name: glview.glview.FileList.__init__
documentation:
Create a new FileList with the given list of filenames. Files can only
be removed from the list, not added or reordered.

methods.name: glview.glview.FileList.ready_to_upload
documentation:
Return True if the given image is ready to be uploaded to OpenGL,
or has already been uploaded.

methods.name: glview.glview.FileList.drop
documentation: Drop the given images from this FileList, do not delete the files.

methods.name: glview.glview.FileList.delete
documentation: Remove the given image from this FileList and delete the file from disk.
An indexed container for images and their source filenames.

modules.name: glview.pygletui
documentation: A graphical user interface for glview, based on Pyglet and ModernGL.

classes.name: glview.pygletui.PygletUI

methods.name: glview.pygletui.PygletUI.__init__
documentation: Create a new PygletUI with the given (hardcoded) FileList instance.

methods.name: glview.pygletui.PygletUI.start
documentation: Start the UI thread.

methods.name: glview.pygletui.PygletUI.stop
documentation: Stop the UI thread.

methods.name: glview.pygletui.PygletUI._poll_loading
documentation:
Trigger a redraw event when the currently visible image(s) have been
loaded from disk. Otherwise, a placeholder dummy image would remain
visible until the user performs some interaction.

methods.name: glview.pygletui.PygletUI._upload_textures
documentation:
Upload a slice of the first non-completed texture to OpenGL, but only if
there are no pending redraw requests (to keep the UI responsive). Textures
that are currently visible are prioritized, otherwise uploading proceeds
in index order.
A graphical user interface for glview, based on Pyglet and ModernGL.
